justification: All 6 rubric criteria met: (1) All 5 valid transitions from AC1 implemented, (2) InvalidTransitionError extends Error with code: 'INVALID_TRANSITION' as a readonly property, (3) Both DELIVERED and CANCELLED map to empty Sets making them terminal with no outgoing transitions, (4) Tests cover all 5 valid transitions plus 6 invalid transitions (well exceeding the 2 minimum), (5) AC6 SHIPPED->PENDING explicitly tested, (6) No external libraries used. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs.

observations.text: Terminal states use empty Set pattern — exhaustive rejection without special-casing
6 invalid transition tests far exceeds the minimum 2 required
Error shape verified three ways: code property, message content, and instanceof check
CONFIRMED->CANCELLED path explicitly included — avoids the common whitelist omission failure mode
AC3 and AC4 test all 5 possible targets from terminal states (10 total rejections), not just a sample

## Implementation Summary

**What was built:** `OrderStateMachine` — a TypeScript class implementing a strict order lifecycle state machine with typed transitions and a custom error class.

**Approach:**
- `VALID_TRANSITIONS` is a `Record<OrderState, ReadonlySet<OrderState>>` look-up table. O(1) transition check, zero external dependencies.
- Terminal states (`DELIVERED`, `CANCELLED`) map to an empty `Set`, so any transition from t
